@startuml
interface Printer {
    + void print(Document)
}

class SimplePrinter implements Printer {
    - Document currentDocument
    
    + setDocument(doc : Document): void
    + getDocument(): Document
    + clearCurrentDoc()
    
    # performPrint(documentToBePrinted: Document): boolean
        note right of this method 
            This protected helper function performs actual printing logic. It returns true if successful or false otherwise; used internally by public methods like 'print()' which handle error checking before calling here directly without any additional checks themselves since they already did so earlier during their execution flow within themselfs prior invoking us now at runtime environment where we're currently executing our own implementation details accordingly based upon what was passed into those parameters previously mentioned above just moments ago... etcetera ad infinitum until finally reaching end state desired outcome achieved successfully! :)  
        	endnote
        
    	# checkIfReadyForPrintingOperationInProgressYetOrNotAlreadyDoneBeforehandElseThrowExceptionOtherwiseContinueNormallyAsExpectedWithoutAnyIssuesAtAllEverAgainForeverAndBeyondTooMuchFunHuh? (): Boolean throws IllegalStateException{ /* ... */ } // Just kidding about name but you got idea ;) 
    
     	
     	 ## Protected Helper Method For Checking Whether Current State Allows Proceeding With Next Step In Sequence Of Operations Required To Complete Task Successfully Without Errors Occurring Along Way That Would Prevent Completion Entirely Otherwise Result Could Be Unpredictable And Potentially Dangerous Depending On Context Where Used So Always Make Sure You Know What Your Doing Before Attempt
